question_html: In the word ‘leaflet’, the suffix, ‘let’ refers to-
Having finished the task, ______.
The stone hit him on ____ shoulder.
In the word ‘Autograph’, the prefix ‘auto’ refers to –
While walking in the field, ______.
options_html: a dead cow was seen by me
I was seen by a dead cow

question_html: He tried ______ to win the prize.

“I’m feeling _____ better today.” –

Which is the appropriate determiner here?
Find out the correct sentence.
options_html: Crossing the street, a car almost struck us.
As we crossed the street, a car almost struck us.
A car almost struck us, crossing the street.
Having crossed the street, a car almost struck us.
correct_answer_html: As we crossed the street, a car almost struck us.

question_html: Which of the following sentences is correct?
options_html: She joined together with the team.

question_html: There is _____ fish on the menu tonight.
There isn’t _____ sugar left in the jar; we need to buy some.
Choose the sentence that contains a dangling modifier.
options_html: While reading the book, I found a mysterious note.
After cleaning the house, the windows gleamed.
Driving through the city, a billboard caught my eye.
She solved the puzzle after a long time.
correct_answer_html: Driving through the city, a billboard caught my eye.
